summary: Pristine soundboard upgrade captures a smoking show highlighted by Scarlet>Fire, Fire on the Mountain, Birdsong and an amusing Estimated jam.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear, upgraded soundboard quality that far exceeds the audience tapes. Musically the concert shines with a blistering Scarlet > Fire sequence, soaring Fire on the Mountain (complete with Mickey Hart’s talking drum), mesmerizing Birdsong and a hilariously wild Estimated jam featuring Bob Weir’s screams. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the best of that tour despite a few mixed comments about set‑list quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive remarks on both musical performance (standout songs, high energy) and recording quality (pristine soundboard upgrade); majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as a pristine upgrade over audience recordings; clear, crisp mix with no noted dropouts or muffling.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy, seamless flow with both nights ‘smoking’; highlighted jams and a memorable talking‑drum segment on Fire.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned as frustrated by Bob’s style during the Estimated jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: His screams during the Estimated jam were called “absolutely hilarious” and added to the fun of the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s talking drum on Fire on the Mountain received specific praise.
